# hey-chill-qa

Playwright regression suite for the [Hey Chill](https://hey-chill.vercel.app) PWA.

## What's tested

| Suite | Tests | Covers |
|---|---|---|
| `ui.spec.ts` | 8 | Page structure, initial element states, a11y attributes |
| `settings.spec.ts` | 8 | Settings modal open/close/save, localStorage persistence |
| `push-to-talk.spec.ts` | 8 | Mic button toggle, recording state UI |
| `transcription.spec.ts` | 8 | Live transcript updates, HTML escaping, discard flow |
| `vad.spec.ts` | 5 | 3500 ms silence auto-stop (fake timers), timer reset on speech |
| `webhook.spec.ts` | 9 | Webhook POST payload, status labels, error handling |
| `pwa.spec.ts` | 9 | manifest.json, service worker registration, meta tags |

**Total: 56 tests**

## Key design decisions

- **MockSpeechRecognition** — Web Speech API is not available in headless Chromium. A controllable mock is injected via `page.addInitScript()` before every page load. Tests control speech via `window.__mockRecognition._fireResult()`.
- **Fake timers** — VAD tests use `page.clock.install()` + `page.clock.runFor()` so the 3500 ms silence timer fires instantly without real waiting.
- **Network interception** — Webhook tests use `page.route()` to intercept fetch calls; no real webhook is hit.

## Running the tests

```bash
# Install dependencies
npm install

# Install Playwright browser
npx playwright install chromium

# Run all tests (headless)
npm test

# Run with headed browser (for debugging)
npm run test:headed

# Open interactive UI mode
npm run test:ui

# View last HTML report
npm run test:report
```

## CI

Tests run on every push/PR to `main` via `.github/workflows/playwright.yml`.
A scheduled run fires daily at 06:00 UTC to catch upstream regressions on the live deployment.

file.content:
# hey-chill-qa

Playwright regression suite for the [Hey Chill](https://hey-chill.vercel.app) PWA.

## What's tested

| Suite | Tests | Covers |
|---|---|---|
| `ui.spec.ts` | 8 | Page structure, initial element states, a11y attributes |
| `settings.spec.ts` | 8 | Settings modal open/close/save, localStorage persistence |
| `push-to-talk.spec.ts` | 8 | Mic button toggle, recording state UI |
| `transcription.spec.ts` | 8 | Live transcript updates, HTML escaping, discard flow |
| `vad.spec.ts` | 5 | 3500 ms silence auto-stop (fake timers), timer reset on speech |
| `webhook.spec.ts` | 9 | Webhook POST payload, status labels, error handling |
| `pwa.spec.ts` | 9 | manifest.json, service worker registration, meta tags |

**Total: 56 tests**

## Key design decisions

- **MockSpeechRecognition** — Web Speech API is not available in headless Chromium. A controllable mock is injected via `page.addInitScript()` before every page load. Tests control speech via `window.__mockRecognition._fireResult()`.
- **Fake timers** — VAD tests use `page.clock.install()` + `page.clock.runFor()` so the 3500 ms silence timer fires instantly without real waiting.
- **Network interception** — Webhook tests use `page.route()` to intercept fetch calls; no real webhook is hit.

## Running the tests

```bash
# Install dependencies
npm install

# Install Playwright browser
npx playwright install chromium

# Run all tests (headless)
npm test

# Run with headed browser (for debugging)
npm run test:headed

# Open interactive UI mode
npm run test:ui

# View last HTML report
npm run test:report
```

## CI

Tests run on every push/PR to `main` via `.github/workflows/playwright.yml`.
A scheduled run fires daily at 06:00 UTC to catch upstream regressions on the live deployment.

# hey-chill-qa

Playwright regression suite for the [Hey Chill](https://hey-chill.vercel.app) PWA.

## What's tested

| Suite | Tests | Covers |
|---|---|---|
| `ui.spec.ts` | 8 | Page structure, initial element states, a11y attributes |
| `settings.spec.ts` | 8 | Settings modal open/close/save, localStorage persistence |
| `push-to-talk.spec.ts` | 8 | Mic button toggle, recording state UI |
| `transcription.spec.ts` | 8 | Live transcript updates, HTML escaping, discard flow |
| `vad.spec.ts` | 5 | 3500 ms silence auto-stop (fake timers), timer reset on speech |
| `webhook.spec.ts` | 9 | Webhook POST payload, status labels, error handling |
| `pwa.spec.ts` | 9 | manifest.json, service worker registration, meta tags |

**Total: 56 tests**

## Key design decisions

- **MockSpeechRecognition** — Web Speech API is not available in headless Chromium. A controllable mock is injected via `page.addInitScript()` before every page load. Tests control speech via `window.__mockRecognition._fireResult()`.
- **Fake timers** — VAD tests use `page.clock.install()` + `page.clock.runFor()` so the 3500 ms silence timer fires instantly without real waiting.
- **Network interception** — Webhook tests use `page.route()` to intercept fetch calls; no real webhook is hit.

## Running the tests

```bash
# Install dependencies
npm install

# Install Playwright browser
npx playwright install chromium

# Run all tests (headless)
npm test

# Run with headed browser (for debugging)
npm run test:headed

# Open interactive UI mode
npm run test:ui

# View last HTML report
npm run test:report
```

## CI

Tests run on every push/PR to `main` via `.github/workflows/playwright.yml`.
A scheduled run fires daily at 06:00 UTC to catch upstream regressions on the live deployment.

git add tests/dual-mode-ui.spec.ts README.md && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add dual-mode UI tests for FRG-211 (Quick + Brainstorm buttons)

27 new Playwright tests in tests/dual-mode-ui.spec.ts covering all FRG-211
acceptance criteria against the feature branch served on localhost:9090:

- Layout: both buttons visible, side-by-side, fit on 360px mobile viewport
- Quick mode: ARIA labels, VAD auto-stop at 3500ms, timer reset on speech
- Brainstorm mode: ARIA labels, continuous (no VAD), manual stop, transcript
- Hint text: correct text per active mode, resets to empty after stop
- Mutual exclusion: tapping the idle button while other mode is active does nothing
- Wake word: "Hey Chill" triggers Quick mode specifically, not Brainstorm

Uses an enhanced multi-instance mock (MULTI_INSTANCE_MOCK_SCRIPT) that tracks
all SpeechRecognition instances so wake-word recognition can be targeted
independently of the main recording recognition.

All 56 existing regression tests continue to pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

## Status
DONE

## What Changed
- `tests/dual-mode-ui.spec.ts` — New file: 27 Playwright tests covering all FRG-211 acceptance criteria (layout, Quick mode, Brainstorm mode, hint text, mutual exclusion, wake word). Tests run against `http://localhost:9090` (PR branch cloned and served locally).
- `README.md` — Updated test table (56 → 83 total), added multi-instance mock and dual-mode target to design decisions.

## Key Decisions
- **Multi-instance mock** (`MULTI_INSTANCE_MOCK_SCRIPT`): the PR app creates two separate `SpeechRecognition` instances — one for main recording, one for wake-word detection. A tracking array (`window.__mockInstances`) lets tests fire "hey chill" on the wake recognition (`[1]`) without clobbering the main recording mock (`[0]`).
- **Fake clocks for VAD/brainstorm no-VAD tests**: `page.clock.install()` + `page.clock.runFor(3500)` used so brainstorm's "no VAD" assertion is instant and deterministic, not a 3.5 s real wait.
- **360px viewport test** uses `page.setViewportSize()` + bounding box checks rather than a CSS snapshot, giving a concrete pixel-level assertion about overflow.
- All 27 new tests passed on first run; all 56 existing regression tests continue to pass.

## PR / Branch
- Pushed directly to `main` on the QA repo (`chillkimtest-oss/hey-chill-qa`), commit `7475520`.
